description_long: This cowshed features a broad, low-pitched hip roof clad in reddish-brown shingles, providing ample coverage for the structure below. The main body is constructed from light-colored wooden planks, giving it a rustic and functional appearance. The front of the cowshed is open in the center, supported by sturdy wooden posts, allowing easy access for livestock and equipment. On either side of the open bay, there are enclosed sections that likely serve as storage or shelter for animals. The roof overhangs the walls, offering additional protection from the elements. The overall design is practical, with minimal ornamentation, emphasizing utility and durability typical of agricultural buildings.
description: A large, rustic cowshed with a reddish hip roof, open central bay, and light wooden plank walls. The structure is practical and spacious, designed for shelter and easy access.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open central bay supported by wooden posts and enclosed sections on both sides.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the broad hip roof and solid wooden wall with no visible openings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full expanse of the hip roof and continuous wooden wall, with no visible doors or windows.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the hip roof and uninterrupted wooden wall.
attribute_door_style: open central bay with no visible doors; side enclosures may have simple wooden gates or doors not visible in these views
